text.title: Four-Stage Explainability Agent Pipeline
text.summary: Extractor → generator → probe → drift agent pipeline, prompt patterns for KB extraction and AI-answer probing, and critical gotchas.

section.title: Four-Stage Pipeline
section.p: Stage 1 (extractor): reads live product (homepage, docs, OpenAPI spec, changelog, in-app strings), emits structured product-kb.json. Stage 2 (generator): renders KB into public surfaces — llms.txt, schema.org JSON-LD, sitemap, docs "is not" section. Stage 3 (probe): queries ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini with canonical questions and diffs answers against KB. Stage 4 (drift): cron job comparing live site, docs, and KB — opens tickets when fields disagree.

rule.statement: Wire stages 1–2 into CI so a release cannot ship without an updated KB. Run stages 3–4 on weekly cron. Persist KB at .aidocs/product_docs/product-kb.json as shared truth for all faion-net agents.
rule.rationale: Explainability debt accumulates silently after each release. CI enforcement is the only mechanism that makes KB updates a required part of shipping, not an afterthought.

example.code: You extract a structured product KB. Inputs: <homepage url>, <docs root>, <openapi spec>, <changelog>. Output JSON with fields: purpose (1 sentence, no marketing adjectives), capabilities[] (each: name, description, example, status: ga|beta|deprecated, since_version), limits[] (each: scope, rationale, workaround_or_null), use_cases[] (each: persona, job, success_signal), audience (industry, role, company_size), prerequisites[]. Refuse to invent fields. If a source is missing, write "unknown" -- never fabricate.

For each question in <question_set>, query <model> with no system prompt, plain user role. Capture the full answer. Score against <product-kb.json> on three axes: factually_correct (0|1), capability_in_scope (0|1), limit_acknowledged (0|1). Output a markdown table sorted by total score asc. Flag any answer that recommends a capability with status=deprecated.

antipattern.reason: Force a "no adjectives, no superlatives" constraint in the system prompt and lint generated output. These tokens provide no retrieval signal and reduce trust.

antipattern.description: Extractor agent fabricating capabilities to fill empty fields.
antipattern.reason: Refuse-on-unknown must be enforced via prompt and post-hoc validator that cross-checks capability names against the feature flag registry or source code. Invented capabilities create support and legal liability.

antipattern.description: Limit array lost across multi-step pipeline summarization passes.
antipattern.reason: Preserve the limits[] array verbatim across every pipeline stage. Never re-summarize limits — they carry legal and trust-critical precision that compression loses.

antipattern.description: Probe agents producing the same response across runs due to same prompt and cached model state.
antipattern.reason: Vary question phrasing, user role, and timing across probe runs. Stable scores are an artifact of caching, not real representation quality.

antipattern.description: Agent autonomously flipping a capability status to deprecated without human review.
antipattern.reason: Deprecated status removes a capability from AI-mediated discovery and directly impacts revenue. Always require product-owner sign-off; agents propose, humans approve the status change.

antipattern.description: Confidential information (unreleased features, internal vendor names, beta-only flags) in published KB.
antipattern.reason: Capability docs frequently leak internal details. Run a scrubber agent before any external publish — especially before pushing llms.txt or schema.org markup.

text.reference: JSON Schema validator for product-kb.json; wire into pre-commit
